title: 中考六选五 2: Why We Compare Ourselves
passage:
It is natural for teenagers to compare themselves with others. They may compare grades, clothes, appearance, popularity or family background. 1. ______ But too much comparison can quietly damage their confidence.

Comparison is not always harmful. Sometimes it can show us what is possible. If a classmate studies well, we may learn useful habits from him or her. 2. ______ In this case, comparison becomes a source of motivation.

The problem begins when students compare only results, not effort. They see another person’s high score but do not see the hours of practice behind it. 3. ______ This can make them feel that others are simply “better”, while they themselves are not good enough.

Another problem is that comparison often focuses on the wrong things. Social media makes this worse. People usually show their best moments online, not their ordinary problems. 4. ______ Comparing your full life with someone else’s selected moments is unfair.

A healthier way is to compare yourself with your past self. Ask whether you have improved, learned something new or handled a problem better than before. 5. ______ This kind of comparison gives you direction without destroying your self-worth.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: It can even encourage us to work harder when we use it correctly.
As a result, students may make unfair judgments about themselves.
This makes comparison common, especially in school life.
What looks perfect may only be a small part of the whole story.
Progress is usually a better measure than being better than someone else.
Students should stop caring about what other people do completely.
